featurePanels.title: Round Read
items: Round 18 is a short five-game slate shaped by Origin absences and a seven-team bye group.
The cleanest raw edge is Sharks over Broncos because Brisbane lose Reece Walsh, Payne Haas and Pat Carrigan, while Cronulla still keep Nicho Hynes and Braydon Trindall together.
Manly over Parramatta and Newcastle over Dolphins are both spine-and-injury reads rather than form-table locks.
Rabbitohs over Panthers is marked as an upset lean, not an anchor, because Penrith are missing Nathan Cleary, Isaah Yeo, Liam Martin and Isaiah Papali'i.

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: This is not a round to build a big multi after the Panthers and Storm hurt last week.
Panthers still have enough system strength to punish an overconfident Rabbitohs lean.
Broncos v Sharks carries late-mail risk because Xavier Willison still needs the NRL concussion exemption pathway to hold.
Dolphins lose Isaiya Katoa, Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow, Jack Bostock, Selwyn Cobbo, Max Plath and Thomas Flegler, but Newcastle also miss Kalyn Ponga and Bradman Best.

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Protect the positive Mark Hughes Foundation ledger at +$0.53.
Keep Round 18 as a tips board until a price, stake and charity-bet confirmation are recorded.
Treat Sharks as the only serious charity-bet watch candidate if the market offers value.
Do not chase last week's Panthers and Storm losses through a larger stake.

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Check final 24-hour cuts for Panthers v Rabbitohs before kickoff on Friday night.
Monitor Xavier Willison clearance and any late Brisbane forward reshuffle.
Watch Manly's five-eighth Joey Walsh in his first Luke Brooks replacement start.
Confirm no late Dolphins spine shuffle before locking confidence on Knights.

watchlist: Sharks over Broncos is the top confidence tip, but only a price-watch candidate until odds and stake are recorded.
Sea Eagles over Eels leans on Tom Trbojevic, Jamal Fogarty and Parramatta losing Mitchell Moses.
Knights over Dolphins is a home lean against a Dolphins side missing Isaiya Katoa and several Origin players.
Dragons over Wests Tigers is low-confidence because both teams are volatile, but Tigers lose Jarome Luai and Taylan May.
Rabbitohs over Panthers is the upset swing and should not be used as a bet anchor.
confidence: Sharks over Broncos - best raw edge; Brisbane are missing Walsh, Haas, Carrigan and multiple forwards
Sea Eagles over Eels - Manly keep Tom Trbojevic and Fogarty while Parramatta lose Mitchell Moses
Knights over Dolphins - Newcastle at home against a Dolphins spine missing Katoa and Tabuai-Fidow
Dragons over Wests Tigers - home lean with Tigers missing Luai and Taylan May
Rabbitohs over Panthers - upset lean only with Penrith missing Cleary, Yeo, Martin and Papali'i

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 18 is a five-game Origin-week puzzle
lines: As of Wednesday, 1 July 2026, Round 18 team lists are live.
The round runs from Friday 3 July to Sunday 5 July 2026.
The five tips are Rabbitohs, Dragons, Sharks, Sea Eagles and Knights.
The charity ledger remains 3-2 with +$0.53 settled profit because no Round 18 charity bet has been locked.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Best tip does not automatically mean a charity bet
lines: Sharks over Broncos is the clearest card lean, but it is still a price-watch item until a fresh TAB number is recorded.
The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
No Round 18 charity result should be added without a pre-recorded stake, price and explicit charity-bet confirmation.
After the Panthers and Storm misses last week, discipline matters more than trying to win the week back in one swing.

sections.title: Round 18 Tips
sections.heading: Confidence order for the short round
lines: Sharks over Broncos - top pick; Brisbane are missing Reece Walsh, Payne Haas, Pat Carrigan and other forward depth.
Sea Eagles over Eels - Manly have the higher ceiling with Tom Trbojevic and Jamal Fogarty; Parramatta lose Mitchell Moses.
Knights over Dolphins - Newcastle get the home lean while Dolphins are without Isaiya Katoa, Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow, Jack Bostock, Selwyn Cobbo, Max Plath and Thomas Flegler.
Dragons over Wests Tigers - home lean because Tigers are without Jarome Luai and Taylan May, even with Alex Twal back.
Rabbitohs over Panthers - upset lean only; Penrith's Origin outs make the usual Panthers trust less automatic.

sections.title: Round 18 Fixtures
sections.heading: Round 18 fixture board
lines: Panthers v Rabbitohs - Friday 3 July, 8:00pm at CommBank Stadium.
Dragons v Wests Tigers - Saturday 4 July, 5:30pm at St George Venues Jubilee Stadium.
Broncos v Sharks - Saturday 4 July, 7:35pm at Suncorp Stadium.
Eels v Sea Eagles - Sunday 5 July, 2:00pm at CommBank Stadium.
Knights v Dolphins - Sunday 5 July, 4:05pm at McDonald Jones Stadium.
Byes: Raiders, Bulldogs, Titans, Storm, Warriors, Cowboys and Roosters.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: What last week changes in the tipping posture
lines: Panthers cannot be treated as an automatic bounce-back team this week because Cleary, Yeo, Martin and Papali'i are out.
Storm are on the bye, so last week's Manly upset should only feed the Manly v Eels read through Tom Trbojevic and confidence at 4 Pines, not direct form-line overreach.
Rabbitohs and Bulldogs both cashed early Round 17 reads, but Bulldogs are on the bye this week.
Short-round cards are easier to overbet because there are fewer options; keep stake logic separate from tip confidence.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The Round 18 team-list details driving the card
lines: Panthers are without Nathan Cleary, Isaah Yeo, Liam Martin and Isaiah Papali'i; Rabbitohs regain Jack Wighton but lose Cameron Murray and Alex Johnston.
Wests Tigers rest Jarome Luai after delayed concussion symptoms and lose Taylan May to a shoulder injury; Alex Twal returns at lock.
Broncos lose Reece Walsh, Payne Haas and Pat Carrigan to Origin, plus Aublix Tawha, Jack Gosiewski and Josiah Karapani; Sharks lose Addin Fonua-Blake, Blayke Brailey and Briton Nikora.
Parramatta lose Mitchell Moses, with Jonah Pezet coming in; Manly lose Luke Brooks for the season but still field Tom Trbojevic and Jamal Fogarty.

Knights lose Kalyn Ponga and Bradman Best to Origin; Dolphins lose Isaiya Katoa to a fractured wrist and are also missing Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow, Jack Bostock, Selwyn Cobbo, Max Plath and Thomas Flegler.

footerNotes: Built from the Codex-maintained data workflow and updated for Round 18 preview on Wednesday 1 July 2026.
Round 18 charity bet status is no bet locked, so the Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains 3-2 and +$0.53.
Round 17 external slip damage is acknowledged, but it does not move the charity ledger without a locked charity stake.
